title: Increasing, decreasing, and constant cost industries
description: Practice what you have learned about the effect of economies of scale on cost and prices in this exercise.
topic_path: Production, cost, and the perfect competition model
Increasing, decreasing, and constant cost industries

assessment_items.question_content:
Rubber eel toys are produced in a perfectly competitive market. The long-run supply curve for these toys is perfectly elastic. 

**Based on this information, which of the following is true for this industry?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: When more firms enter this market, input prices don’t change.
When more firms enter this market, each firm’s average total cost curve increases.
When more firms enter this market, input prices increase.
An increase in demand in this market increases prices in the long-run.
The marginal cost curve for a firm in this industry decreases as more firms enter.

assessment_items.question_content:
Macarons are produced in a perfectly competitive market. The long-run supply curve for macarons is horizontal. 

**Based on this information, which of the following is true for this industry?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: When more firms enter this market, input prices don’t change.
When more firms enter this market, each firm’s average total cost curve increases.
When more firms enter this market, input prices increase.
An increase in demand in this market increases prices in the long-run.
The marginal cost curve for a firm in this industry decreases as more firms enter.

assessment_items.question_content:
**If shark repellent is produced in a perfectly competitive market and its long-run supply curve is horizontal, which of the following must be true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: When more firms enter this market, input prices don’t change.
When more firms enter this market, each firm’s average total cost curve increases.
When more firms enter this market, input prices increase.
An increase in demand in this market increases prices in the long-run.
The marginal cost curve for a firm in this industry decreases as more firms enter.

assessment_items.question_content:
**If the market for tacos is a perfectly competitive decreasing cost industry, which of the following is also true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: An increase in demand decreases prices in the long run.
An increase in demand increases prices in the long run.
An increase in demand has no effect on prices in the long run.
An increase in demand has no effect on a typical firm’s average total cost in the long run.
An increase in demand increases a typical firm’s average total cost curve.

assessment_items.question_content:
Washi tape is produced in a perfectly competitive market.

**If the long-run supply curve for washi tape is upward sloping, which of the following is true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Washi tape is an increasing cost industry.
Washi tape is a constant cost industry.
Washi tape is a decreasing cost industry.
Washi tape is a parabolic cost industry.
Washi tape is in a diminishing cost industry.

assessment_items.question_content:
Walrus hats are sold in a perfectly competitive industry and its long-run supply curve slopes upward.

**What can be concluded based on this information?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: This is an increasing cost industry.
This is a constant cost industry.
This is a decreasing cost industry.
This is a parabolic cost industry.
This is a diminishing cost industry.

assessment_items.question_content:
Banana nut muffins are produced and sold in a perfectly competitive increasing cost industry. A scientific study showed that eating banana nut muffins leads to a greater ability to make friends, leading to an increase in the demand for banana nut muffins.

**What happens to each firm’s average total cost curve and the price of banana nut muffins in the long run?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Average total cost curves increase; the price of banana nut muffins increases.
Average total cost curves don’t change; the price of banana nut muffins increases.
Average total cost curves decrease; the price of banana nut muffins decrease.
Average total cost curves increase; the price of banana nut muffins doesn’t change.
Average total cost curves don’t change; the price of banana nut muffins doesn’t change.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is true about constant cost industries?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The prices of inputs used to produce a good don’t change when more firms enter the industry.
The long-run supply curve is upward sloping.
The long-run supply curve is downward sloping.
The prices of inputs used to produce a good decrease when more firms enter the industry.
The supply curve for inputs used to produce this good is at least somewhat inelastic.

assessment_items.question_content:
Turkey jerky is produced and sold in a perfectly competitive increasing cost industry and buyers consider it a normal good. The incomes of turkey jerky buyers have increased.

**What happens to each firm’s average total cost curve and the price of turkey jerky in the long run?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Average total cost curves increase; the price of turkey jerky increases.
Average total cost curves don’t change; the price of turkey jerky increases.
Average total cost curves decrease; the price of turkey jerky decrease.
Average total cost curves increase; the price of turkey jerky doesn’t change
Average total cost curves don’t change; the price of turkey jerky doesn’t change.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following happens in the long run when demand increases in a perfectly competitive constant cost industry?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Price doesn’t change.
Firms make economic profits.
Average total costs increase.
Firms become allocatively inefficient
Market quantity doesn’t change.

assessment_items.question_content:
Coffee is produced in a constant cost industry and is sold in a perfectly competitive market.

**If the market demand for this good increased, which of the following will happen in the long run?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The price of coffee stays the same.
Firm’s make economic profits.
Average total costs increase.
Firms become allocatively inefficient.
The quantity of coffee sold in the market doesn’t change.

assessment_items.question_content:
Typical firms in a perfectly competitive decreasing cost industry are currently earning economic profits. 

**What will happen to the number of firms, the market price, and each firm’s marginal cost in the long run?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The number of firms will increase; the market price will decrease; marginal cost will decrease.
The number of firms will increase; the market price will decrease; marginal cost will increase.
The number of firms will decrease; the market price will increase; marginal cost will shift to the right.
The number of firms will stay the same; the market price will stay the same; marginal cost will stay the same.

assessment_items.question_content:
Skin serum is produced in a perfectly competitive increasing cost industry, and firms currently earn positive economic profits.

**What will happen to the number of firms, the market price, and each firm’s marginal cost in the long run?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The number of firms will increase; the market price will increase; marginal cost will increase.
The number of firms will increase; the market price will decrease; marginal cost will decrease.
The number of firms will stay the same; the market price will stay the same; marginal cost will stay the same.
The number of firms will decrease; the market price will decrease; marginal cost will shift down.
The number of firms will decrease; the market price will decrease; marginal cost will decrease.

assessment_items.question_content:
Cheesy crackers are sold in a perfectly competitive market. An increase in the demand for cheesy crackers increased price in the short run, but prices decreased in the long run.

**Which of the following can be concluded based on the information given?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The producers of this good were operating below minimum efficient scale.
An increase in production had no effect on input prices.
The firms in this market competed on the basis of price.
As more firms entered this market, input prices increased.
This is a constant cost industry.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following occurs in the long run if firms in a perfectly competitive constant cost industry are making economic profits?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The market price doesn’t change.
The average total cost curve of a typical firm shifts up.
The marginal cost curve of a typical firm decreases.
The market short-run supply curve doesn’t change.
A typical firm will continue to make economic profits.
